assertion_results.text: Alert thresholds are derived from the stated SLO (99.95% availability) using burn rate calculations, not arbitrary fixed values
assertion_results.evidence: Alert uses error_rate > 0.05 (5%) as threshold. No burn rate calculation. SLO mentioned in comments but not used in the alert expression.

assertion_results.text: Alert rules are in valid Prometheus alerting rule YAML format with groups, rules, expr, for, labels, and annotations
assertion_results.evidence: Valid Prometheus alerting rule YAML format with all required fields.

assertion_results.text: Each alert rule includes severity labels (critical, warning) and a team label for routing
assertion_results.evidence: Severity labels present (critical, warning) but no team label for routing.

assertion_results.text: Every alert annotation includes a runbook link URL pointing to resolution documentation
assertion_results.evidence: No runbook annotations. Only summary and description annotations present.

assertion_results.text: An escalation policy is defined with different response times and notification channels per severity level (PagerDuty for critical, Slack for warning)
assertion_results.evidence: No escalation policy document. Only alert rules YAML generated.

assertion_results.text: Runbook files are generated for each alert containing diagnosis steps, resolution procedures, and escalation instructions
Latency alert uses histogram_quantile for P99 measurement against the 200ms SLO target, not average latency
assertion_results.evidence: Latency alert uses avg(rate(http_request_duration_seconds_sum[5m]) / rate(http_request_duration_seconds_count[5m])) > 0.5. Uses average, not P99. Threshold is 500ms, not 200ms.

assertion_results.text: Error rate alert uses a ratio calculation (errors/total requests) rather than an absolute error count
assertion_results.evidence: Error rate uses ratio: http_requests_total{status=~"5.."} / http_requests_total. However threshold is arbitrary 5%.
